context_pointers.vague_reference: matches the mutation flag behavior described in the Data Structure Integrity section
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The boolean flag 'mutation_prevented' must be exactly true, as per the constraint table which specifies the output rule for failed assignments.

context_pointers.vague_reference: applicable to the function invocation tests in the Core Functions test suite
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Retrieves specific numeric outputs (direct_call=17, zero_args=-Infinity, etc.) associated with the 'callback_invocation' scenario where function arity determines the result.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_errors.json.description: Error objects preserve their domain category and message, thrown messages are reported through a normalized error contract, and failed conversions can be rendered as domain text.

cases.expected_output:
category=type_error
message=Type error? From Rust?!
base_error=true

feature11_binary_memory.json.description: Binary memory operations expose byte lengths, byte contents, view metadata, copy behavior, and normalized failures for invalid or overlapping regions.

feature12_boxed_state.json.description: Opaque stateful values can be created, greeted, mutated, and protected by dynamic borrow/type checks reported with neutral error categories.

feature13_async_results.json.description: Asynchronous callbacks, promises, and worker-backed computations resolve to numeric results or reject with normalized exception categories.

feature1_module_exports.json.description: Values exported at module initialization are visible with the expected primitive, number, object, and callable behavior.

feature2_numbers.json.description: Numeric values crossing the native boundary preserve integer, large integer, floating point, and negative-number behavior as observed by callers.

feature3_string_scripts.json.description: String values can be returned, evaluated as source text, and reported with normalized script error categories when evaluation fails.

feature4_arrays.json.description: Array outputs preserve length and indexed content, and reading the first element returns either that value or an undefined sentinel for an empty array.

feature5_objects.json.description: Object outputs preserve own properties, object immutability operations affect later mutation/deletion attempts, own-property enumeration excludes inherited and symbolic keys, and method invocation uses the receiver object.

feature6_value_stringification.json.description: Values are converted to strings using the host value conversion semantics observable at the boundary.

feature7_type_predicates.json.description: Runtime predicates report the observable kind of values, and strict identity comparison distinguishes equal primitives from distinct object references.

cases.expected_output:
array_buffer=true
data_view=false
uint8_array_as_array_buffer=false
byte_buffer=true
uint8_array_as_buffer=true
array_buffer_as_buffer=false
uint32_array=true
uint16_array_as_uint32=false

feature8_function_calls.json.description: Functions crossing the boundary remain callable, receive the expected arguments and receiver, support construction, expose argument counts/presence, and can capture thrown values as data.

cases.expected_output:
direct_call=17
idiomatic_call=17
zero_args=-Infinity
one_arg=1
two_args=2
three_args=3
four_args=4
heterogeneous=[1,"hello",true]

feature9_dates.json.description: Date values can be created from millisecond timestamps, converted back to timestamps, and classified as valid or invalid with invalid values represented as NaN.

_constraints.output_rule: Routes execution to specific feature block (greeting, numbers, strings, arrays, objects, coercion, types, functions, dates, errors, binary, boxed, async)
Outputs key=value pairs separated by newlines, terminated with a newline character.
Outputs static exported values
Outputs undefined, null, boolean primitives truth values
Outputs generated numeric constants including large integers and floats

_constraints.input_condition: operation=numbers AND scenario=round_trip AND input.value defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs input value and result value
Outputs the created string value

_constraints.input_condition: operation=strings AND scenario=evaluate_script AND input.source is valid JS expression
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs source and calculated result
Outputs length 0 and empty items array
Outputs length 1 and array containing 9000
Outputs JSON representations of empty, number, string, and mixed object scenarios
Outputs before value, result after failed assignment (should not change), and boolean flag
Outputs input kind and comma-separated string result
Outputs boolean truthiness for various array-like instances
Outputs boolean truthiness/identity for various buffer and typed array scenarios
exact_values:
array_buffer=true
data_view=false
uint8_array_as_array_buffer=false
byte_buffer=true
uint8_array_as_buffer=true
array_buffer_as_buffer=false
uint32_array=true
uint16_array_as_uint32=false

_constraints.input_condition: operation=functions AND scenario=returned_incrementer AND input.value defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs input value and incremented result (input + 1)
Outputs results for direct calls, idiomatic calls, and heterogeneous arrays
exact_values:
direct_call=17
idiomatic_call=17
zero_args=-Infinity
one_arg=1
two_args=2
three_args=3
four_args=4
heterogeneous=[1,"hello",true]

_constraints.input_condition: operation=dates AND scenario=create_from_millis AND input.millis defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs milliseconds and UTC formatted string

_constraints.input_condition: operation=errors AND scenario=create_generic AND input.message defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs error category and message

_constraints.input_condition: operation=errors AND scenario=create_type AND input.message defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs type error category, message, and base_error flag
Outputs byte length and 4 zero values
Outputs read/write values for specific 16-byte buffer operations
Outputs lock/borrow operations on specific 16-byte buffer

_constraints.input_condition: operation=boxed AND scenario=greet AND input.name defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs name and generated greeting

_constraints.input_condition: operation=boxed AND scenario=ref_greet AND input.name defined
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs name and generated greeting (same as greet scenario)
Outputs resolved integer result (1+2)
If input.value is missing, behavior is undefined/not tested in provided cases; default assumption is value is required per logic
input.millis is required; if missing output behavior undefined
input.value is required per test case 'value': 41; output depends on it
